meta.title: Free Construction Invoice Template | Download & Send Instantly
meta.description: Create professional construction invoices. Perfect for general contractors and builders to track milestones, labor, materials, and change orders.

blocks.title: Construction Invoice Template
blocks.subtitle: Stop struggling with confusing spreadsheets. Use our free construction invoice template to bill for materials, subcontractors, and labor with complete clarity.

blocks.authorBio: Jane is a certified CPA with over 15 years of experience in small business accounting.

blocks.heading: The Complexity of Construction Billing
blocks.text: Construction projects are notorious for complex billing cycles. Between initial deposits, progress payments, subcontractor fees, and unexpected change orders, keeping your cash flow organized is a massive challenge. A specialized construction invoice provides the exact structure needed to ensure transparency with your clients and guarantee you get paid for every nail driven and hour worked.

summaryItems: Always reference the original contract or estimate number.
Use progress billing (e.g., 25% Completion Milestone) for large projects.
Clearly isolate change orders from the original scope of work.
Ensure compliance with local retainage laws if applicable.

features.title: Progress Billing Ready
features.description: Easily set up your invoice to bill for a percentage of completion rather than hourly rates.

features.title: Change Order Transparency
features.description: Add specific line items for approved change orders to prevent disputes over the final bill.

features.title: Send from the Job Site
features.description: Don't wait until you get back to the office. Generate and email a PDF invoice instantly from your phone.

steps.title: Include Contractor Licensing
steps.description: Display your legal business name, address, and state/local contractor license numbers prominently at the top.

steps.title: Specify the Billing Period or Milestone
steps.description: Clearly state what is being billed (e.g., 'Draw 2: Framing & Roofing Completion' or 'Billing Period: Aug 1 - Aug 30').

steps.title: Itemize Clearly
steps.description: Separate your costs into logical categories: Labor, Materials, Subcontractor Fees, and Equipment Rentals.

steps.title: Account for Retainage (If Applicable)
steps.description: If your commercial contract requires a 5% or 10% retainage (held back until final completion), add a negative line item to deduct this from the current payment due.

blocks.tip: Attach a 'Schedule of Values' (SOV) to your progress invoices for commercial projects. This provides the property owner with a detailed breakdown of the total contract sum into its various components, making invoice approval much faster.

questions.question: What is progress billing in construction?
questions.description: Progress billing is an invoicing method where you bill for the percentage of work completed during a specific period, rather than waiting until the entire project is finished.

questions.question: Should I include receipts for materials on a construction invoice?
questions.description: If you are working on a 'Time and Materials' (T&M) contract, yes, you should attach copies of material receipts. If you are working on a fixed-price contract, detailed itemization is usually sufficient without attaching raw receipts.

blocks.heading: Ready to bill your client?
blocks.subtext: Use our free invoice generator to create a professional construction invoice in seconds.
